links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: left|thumb|Preserved male specimen at Naturalis Biodiversity Center The species inhabits tropical moist lowland primary forest and secondary forest up to 1,000 meters above sea level. It is assessed as near threatened under the IUCN with populations believed to be decreasing due to habitat loss, deforestation, hunting and the cage bird trade
